description:
You are a storage administrator who needs to free up space by compressing an existing directory of rotated log files.

Initial system state that you should find when you open the terminal:
• The directory /home/user/storage/logs already exists and contains exactly three plain-text log files:
  1. /home/user/storage/logs/app-2023-09-01.log  
  2. /home/user/storage/logs/app-2023-09-02.log  
  3. /home/user/storage/logs/app-2023-09-03.log  
  (The files are small; their specific contents are irrelevant to you.)

• The directory /home/user/archives exists but is empty.

Your tasks:
1. Create a compressed tar archive called logs_backup.tar.xz that contains the entire contents (and only the contents) of /home/user/storage/logs.  
   ‑ The archive must be placed directly in /home/user/archives.  
   ‑ The archive must preserve the relative paths, so when someone lists the archive they should see:
     ./app-2023-09-01.log  
     ./app-2023-09-02.log  
     ./app-2023-09-03.log

2. Immediately after creating the archive, generate a plain-text report file called compression_report.txt in /home/user/archives with the following exact, line-oriented format (four lines total, no extra spaces or blank lines):
   archive_name: logs_backup.tar.xz  
   original_files: 3  
   archive_size_bytes: <SIZE>  
   compression_method: xz  

   Replace <SIZE> with the exact byte size of /home/user/archives/logs_backup.tar.xz as reported by stat --format=%s. Do not add any thousands separators or units—just the raw number.

Important output constraints the automated test will check:
• /home/user/archives/logs_backup.tar.xz must exist and be a valid tar.xz archive that untars cleanly.  
• Listing the archive with “tar -tf /home/user/archives/logs_backup.tar.xz” must show exactly and only the three files above, each prefixed with ./  
• /home/user/archives/compression_report.txt must contain exactly four lines in the order and format shown, with a numeric value on the third line.

You only need ordinary user privileges to complete this assignment.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the agent starts:
1. /home/user/storage/logs/app-2023-09-01.log  
   Content: “Dummy log 1\n”
2. /home/user/storage/logs/app-2023-09-02.log  
   Content: “Dummy log 2\n”
3. /home/user/storage/logs/app-2023-09-03.log  
   Content: “Dummy log 3\n”

Directories already present:
• /home/user/storage/logs  (mode 755, owned by user)  
• /home/user/archives      (mode 755, owned by user)

Files that must exist after successful task completion:
A. /home/user/archives/logs_backup.tar.xz  
   - Must be a tar archive compressed with xz.  
   - Inside the archive, “tar -Jtf” must list exactly:
     ./app-2023-09-01.log
     ./app-2023-09-02.log
     ./app-2023-09-03.log

B. /home/user/archives/compression_report.txt  
   Exact format (example with fictitious size 512 bytes):
     archive_name: logs_backup.tar.xz
     original_files: 3
     archive_size_bytes: 512
     compression_method: xz

   Line endings: UNIX (LF).

Automated test procedure:
1. Confirm that the archive file exists.  
2. Run “tar -Jtf /home/user/archives/logs_backup.tar.xz” and compare output lines with the expected three paths.  
3. Extract the archive to a temp dir and verify the extracted files match the original names and their contents equal the dummy strings above.  
4. Read /home/user/archives/compression_report.txt and ensure:
   - Line 1 is exactly “archive_name: logs_backup.tar.xz”  
   - Line 2 is exactly “original_files: 3”  
   - Line 3 matches regex “archive_size_bytes: [0-9]+” and the number equals the file size obtained via stat.  
   - Line 4 is exactly “compression_method: xz”  
   - File contains no extra lines or trailing blanks.
name: task_000000_baf4fbca
